name: Eggplant Sichuan Style
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Peanut or corn oil (or more as needed)
1/4 cup Chopped water chestnuts (peeled, preferably fresh)
1 tablespoon Toasted black sesame seeds (for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut eggplant into 1/2-by-2-inch strips. If using Italian eggplants, peel the skin.
2. In a colander, toss eggplant with salt; drain for 30 minutes. Gently squeeze to remove excess water and pat dry with paper towels.
3. Mix soy sauce, sugar, and chicken stock in a small bowl.
4. Preheat wok over high heat. Add 3 tablespoons of oil, tilting the wok to coat sides.
5. When hot, add one layer of eggplant and stir-fry until seared and tender (about 3 minutes). Remove to colander and drain juices in a bowl.
6. Cook remaining eggplant in same manner, adding more oil if needed.
7. Reheat wok over medium-high heat. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil, ginger, garlic, and chile; cook gently but do not brown.
8. Add water chestnuts and half of the green onions; stir-fry together for 5 seconds.
9. Increase to high heat, add reserved soy sauce mixture and eggplant juices; bring to a boil.
10. Return cooked eggplant and toss quickly over high heat until most of the sauce is reduced and absorbed into the eggplant (about 1-to-2 minutes).
11. Fold in vinegar and sesame oil. Transfer to serving dish and top with remaining green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot or cold.
